channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for the longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Naval Academy removes nearly 400 books from library in new DEI purge ordered by Hegseth's office
item.description: U.S. officials say the U.S. Naval Academy has removed nearly 400 books from its library after being told by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s office to review and get rid of books that promote diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration halts dozens of research grants at Princeton University
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has halted dozens of research grants at Princeton University, the latest Ivy League school to see its federal money threatened in a pressure campaign targeting the nation’s top universities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mass layoffs are underway at the nation's public health agencies
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department received notices Tuesday that their jobs were being eliminated, part of a sweeping overhaul designed to vastly shrink the agencies responsible for protecting and promoting Americans’ health.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans, Democrats trade barbs in heated hearing on activist judges blocking Trump agenda
item.description: The House Judiciary Committee held a hearing on judges blocking the Trump administration's agenda on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin defends deporting accused MS-13 leader to El Salvador amid backlash
item.description: The Trump administration is remaining firm in its stance to arrest a man following a report from The Atlantic that the federal attorneys said that there was an “administrative error.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Tonight's elections are a Trump, Musk referendum
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vermont lawmaker defends call for 'picking winners and losers' as indictment of 'rigged' economy toward rich
item.description: A Vermont state representative is under fire from Republicans for urging 'the right people' be allowed to 'win' economically versus the billionaire class.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto execs making last-ditch tariff appeal to the White House
item.description: The heads of auto companies Ford, GM and Stellantis are making a last-minute effort to appeal to the White House, NewsNation has learned.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans torpedo Johnson effort to block proxy voting for new parents
item.description: The House GOP battle over proxy voting for new parents escalated to a full-blown procedural war Tuesday that will have ramifications for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) ability to control his razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge in long-running lawsuit declines to block the use of Georgia's voting system
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge has declined to block the use of Georgia's electronic voting system in a long-running lawsuit alleging that the system is vulnerable to attack and has operational issues that could deprive voters of their constitutional rights. U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat: DDHQ
item.description: The race drew national attention and was one of the first elections to gauge voters' feelings about Trump's first few months in office.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pick for Joint Chiefs chairman vows to be apolitical and addresses Signal chat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s nominee to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine, told senators Tuesday that he understands he is an unknown and unconventional nominee — but that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican Randy Fine wins Mike Waltz's Florida District 6 seat: DDHQ
item.description: During November's presidential election, President Donald Trump won the district on Florida’s eastern coast by 30 points.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump steps up efforts to exert control over DC
item.description: President Trump is growing more aggressive in his posture towards Washington, D.C, threatening to exert more control over the local affairs of the nation's capital.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Entire staff at federal agency that funds libraries and museums put on leave
item.description: The staff of the Institute of Museum and Library Services was placed on administrative leave Monday morning, following a meeting between IMLS leadership and DOGE staff.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong, MPR News

item.title: Crime, housing and Cuomo in focus in race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates on the campaign trail for New York City mayor are zeroing in on crime and affordability, as a new accusation against former Gov. Andrew Cuomo bubbles to the surface.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota insurance costs could continue to rise under new Trump tariffs
item.description: Tariffs are taxes on goods imported from other countries, but these new taxes are not just impacting the prices of our T-shirts and televisions. Tariffs may even lead to higher insurance rates here at home.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court race draws national attention, record out-of-state money
item.description: Zac Schultz, a reporter with PBS Wisconsin’s “Here & Now” program, has been closely covering the race and joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition with the highlights.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: Just 38 percent of respondents said they approve of Trump's approach to trade negotiations with other countries.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Harris pleaded with Biden for speedy endorsement
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris pleaded with former President Biden to endorse her White House bid on the same day as his historic move to end his 2024 reelection bid, according to exclusive excerpts from a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: How Biden's frailties hampered Harris
item.description: Democratic Party officials and White House staffers were well aware of former President Biden’s frailties ahead of his choice to end his ill-fated reelection bid last summer, according to exclusive excerpts of a new book released to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FCC chair opens investigation into Disney and ABC over DEI practices
item.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr said he wants a review all of Disney's DEI practices — both past and present — to determine whether the company has ever violated any FCC regulations.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump won't rule out military force to take Greenland
item.description: In an interview with NBC News, Trump refused to rule out military force to acquire Greenland. His remarks come a day after Vice President JD Vance's scaled back visit to the island.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: State Republicans say Sigal Chattah nominated for Nevada U.S. Attorney post
item.description: Nevada Republicans are saying Sigal Chattah has been nominated to serve as interim U.S. Attorney overseeing Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
